content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are playing a game.

Chef has NNN monsters (numbered 111 through NNN). For each valid iii, the iii-th monster has a value V1,iV_{1,i}V1,i​ and it is either in attack mode or defense mode. The initial states of the monsters are described by a string D1D_1D1​: for each valid iii, the iii-th monster is in attack mode if the iii-th character of D1D_1D1​ is 'A' or in defense mode if the iii-th character is 'D'.

Chefina has MMM monsters (numbered 111 through MMM). Their values are V2,1,V2,2,…,V2,MV_{2,1}, V_{2,2}, \ldots, V_{2,M}V2,1​,V2,2​,…,V2,M​ and their initial states are described by a string D2D_2D2​ in the same way.

In the game, the players alternate turns; Chef plays first. During each turn, the current player may either end the game immediately or do the following: choose one of their own living monsters in attack mode, choose a monster of the other player in defense mode, attack and kill this defending monster and change the attacking monster to defense mode. Note that once a monster is in defense mode, it never changes to attack mode.

Each player is trying to maximise the difference X−YX-YX−Y, where XXX is the sum of values of this player's living monsters (in both modes, but only if they are alive) and YYY is the sum of values of the opponent's living monsters. Assuming that both players are playing optimally, calculate the sum of values of Chef's living monsters minus the sum of values of Chefina's living monsters after the game ends.
constraints: initially, Chefina has at least one monster in defense mode
D1D_1D1​ and D2D_2D2​ contain only characters 'A' and 'D'
1≤Vi,j≤1091 \le V_{i,j} \le 10^91≤Vi,j​≤109 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
initially, Chefina has exactly one monster in defense mode and Chef has no monsters in defense mode
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers V1,1,V1,2,…,V1,NV_{1,1}, V_{1,2}, \ldots, V_{1,N}V1,1​,V1,2​,…,V1,N​.
The third line contains a single string D1D_1D1​ with length NNN.
The fourth line contains MMM space-separated integers V2,1,V2,2,…,V2,MV_{2,1}, V_{2,2}, \ldots, V_{2,M}V2,1​,V2,2​,…,V2,M​.
The fifth line contains a single string D2D_2D2​ with length MMM.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the sum of values of Chef's living monsters minus the sum of values of Chefina's living monsters after the game ends.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef attacks Chefina's first monster with his second monster. On the next turn, Chefina decides to end the game.
